description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, codequality, and security (added by the Dependency‑Scanning template). Add a build job using ruby:2.6 that runs `gem install bundler --no-document` then `bundle update` and publishes Gemfile.lock as an artifact. Add multiple rspec‑rubyX‑railsY jobs in the test stage, each using the specified ruby image, executing a before_script that prints ruby version, echoes the RSPEC_RAILS_VERSION variable, installs bundler, sets BUNDLE_GEMFILE, runs `bundle install --jobs=$(nproc) --path="../cache/bundle"` and `bundle list`, caches the `cache/` directory with a key of the job name, and then runs `bundle exec rake spec`; each job sets RSPEC_RAILS_VERSION to the appropriate Rails version. Add a rubocop job in the codequality stage using ruby:3.1 that installs rubocop, rubocop‑performance and rubocop‑rails and then runs `rubocop`. The pipeline includes the Security/Dependency‑Scanning template to add a security stage.
